title: Mioca — let go, and the business keeps running (coming soon)
description: An unattended business OS. Mioca decides and executes across acquisition, support, delivery, and improvement. Numbers measured deterministically, published with tamper-evident signatures. Waitlist opening soon.
ogTitle: Mioca — a business OS that runs itself
ogDescription: Let go of the wheel, the business keeps running. From proposing to shipping, measuring, and revising.
eyebrow: the business OS that runs unattended
headline:
Let go of the wheel,
the business keeps running.
lead: Mioca decides and executes across acquisition, support, delivery, and improvement. You touch it only a little each week. Every number is measured deterministically and published with a tamper-evident signature.
waitlistNote: Not for sale yet. There's no checkout on this page. Join the waitlist and we'll tell you first when it opens — and share indicative pricing before anyone else.

console.title: Autonomous activity
console.foot: * This is an illustration (coming soon). After launch, this shows real data from the internal ledger.

capHeading:
Start as one person.
Grow into your core OS.
capLead: Today, for one person's hands-off. Tomorrow, as an organization's backbone. The same system grows with you.

caps.title: Executes autonomously
caps.body: From acquisition to support, delivery, and improvement, Mioca decides and acts. You just hand it the goal.

caps.title: Proves it deterministically
caps.body: Every number is reproducible from the same input and signed for tamper-detection. Hand your results straight to an audit.

caps.title: Stays under control
caps.body: High-risk actions always pass a human approval gate. Even running itself, control stays in your hands.

how: Read the market and the response
Propose the next move — with a hypothesis
Wait for your approval on the calls that matter
Measure the result and record it
Rewrite the strategy, then go again
proofLabel: LIVE TELEMETRY · Proof of unattended operation
proofTitle: See it running, as it runs.

proof.heading: * Coming soon: this is replaced by real ledger data after launch. For now, it explains the mechanism.
proof.body: Every number is tallied deterministically from the internal ledger. The LLM writes none of it. Recompute the signature from the same input and anyone can detect tampering.
points: Decisions and shipments are logged — when, what, and why
Deliverables are signed, so tampering is detectable
Deterministic steps return the same output for the same input
Low-confidence output is held for a human, not published

pricing.note: Pricing is still being set. Waitlist members get indicative pricing before it's finalized. You can't buy or pay on this page yet.

faq.a: The date is still being set. Waitlist members hear about the launch and pricing first.
You set the direction and approve the calls that matter. Everything else — proposing, building, shipping, measuring, revising — the machine runs itself. We only claim what it can actually do.
Decide what to aim for, and approve high-risk actions. It isn't built around manual selling or hand-posting.

faq.q: How does this relate to the existing Mioca?
faq.a: Those are among the things Mioca produces. This OS runs the producing, shipping, and improving itself.

closingBody: We'll tell you first when it opens. Indicative pricing goes to the waitlist first, too.
